name: Ace of Swords
interpretation: A jester drives a sword straight through the head of a doll of a King. She is not afraid to shock, and always ready to take down a tyrant.
image_description:
A jester winks as she pierces the head of a doll with a sword.

The jester is off on the left side of the card. She wears a three-pointed red and green hat with yellow bells on it. Her jacket is half green and half patterned with red and blue diamonds and she wears a yellow collar. The sword, in the center of the card, has a red blade and yellow and green hilt. It pokes through the doll's head where the nose would be. The doll is a crude rag doll with a yellow crown and a purple cape with the suggestion of an ermine collar.

Surrounding the jester's hand on the hilt of the sword, there is a wavy blue circle within another ring of yellow and red spikes. In the air around the sword, there are 25 pieces of confetti in red, yellow, orange, green, and blue.

name: Two of Swords
interpretation: Most of the time, jesters didn't need to entertain, so they would take on other household duties. Here an off-duty jester dreams while watching the master's hounds.
image_description: A jester dozes slouched in a chair, with legs crossed and head nodding down. A blue dog pulls one of his slippers off his foot. Four more dogs sleep on the floor in front of the jester: one red, one orange, one yellow, and one green. The jester's pointed hat hangs from a post next to the chair. Above the jester, two naked cherubs hang down, each holding a sword. The two swords cross in an X. The jester has yellow hair and wears a white outfit, with red and green stripes on the sleeves, a wide blue collar, and pointy red and green slippers. The floor is black.

name: Three of Swords
interpretation: A trio of jesters interrupt a presumably sedate banquet with a good olde-tymey jam band.
image_description: A jester, playing a lute, strides down in the middle of a banquet table, stepping his right foot into a pie. By his sides, two more jesters sit at the table, one playing a tambourine and the other playing a recorder. On the table, there is also a roast fowl with three knives stuck in it, and various dirty plates. The jesters wear mainly yellow. The table is blue and there are rainbow-colored music notes in the air. The jester on the left side wears polka dots and a red and blue striped hat with a single point ending in a yellow bell. She plays a tambourine decorated with a six-petaled daisy. The jester on the right side has a curled red hat with yellow buttons all the way up his side. He points one of his red-slipped feet in the air as he plays his recorder. The jester on the table has a three-pointed red and purple hat with bells, and he also has bells flying from his collar on purple and green ribbons. His pants are green and decorated with red hearts and his shoes are purple with bells on the toes. His orange and green lute has a flower motif on the front.

name: Four of Swords
interpretation: Here the jester appears as an advisor to the King, enjoying a level of intimacy with political influence that few other positions provide.
image_description:
A jester leans down to whisper into a king's ear.

The king sits on a throne whose legs are made of four upward-pointing swords. He wears a long red robe, green boots with red crosses on them, and a green tunic with an image of a golden eagle. He has blue hair and a beard, and a four-pointed crown. The jester wears a four-pointed hat of blue, orange, red, and green, with yellow bells. His jumpsuit is half purple and half red with a pattern of gold suns. He wears white shoes with long curly toes and bells on the end. The floor is tiled in black and white checkers and there is an orange semicircle behind the throne.

name: Five of Swords
interpretation: In her spare time, a jester studies the stars. A hobby like this is a luxury of being in a wealthy household and having time on one's hands, but who knows what the jester will gain by it on earth?
image_description:
A smiling jester peers through a telescope.

The jester has orange hair and wears a blue robe covered in yellow five-pointed stars, with a matching conical hat. The telescope is green, blue, red, purple, and yellow, and the jester's eye is visible in the end. The telescope is supported by a tripod whose legs are shaped like red sword blades. It stands on a table covered in a green cloth, among various books and pieces of paper. There is a green quill pen in a blue inkwell, a small red knight, and a compass with a red sword as one of its points. Cut-outs of planets and stars hang from strings behind the jester.

name: Six of Swords
interpretation: Turning to artistic pursuits, a jester creates a puppet resembling himself.
image_description:
A winking jester stands in front of a table of art supplies and paints the face of a puppet.

The jester wears a three-pointed red and green hat with bells. His outfit is half green and half patterned with red and blue diamonds. The hand-puppet has the same outfit. On the table in front of the jester, there is a palette, two more paint brushes, a needle in a heart-shaped pincushion, and two pairs of scissors lying on pieces of the same fabric from the costumes. A chair behind the jester has red cushions with a pattern of golden birds. The floor is dark blue.

name: Seven of Swords
interpretation: Jesters also had duties in wartime. Here a jester carries a message across a battleground at night.
image_description: A jester dressed in black tip-toes through a field, carrying a scroll. The jester walks toward the right side of the card, looking back over his shoulder. His outfit is mainly black, with some red trim and a short pointed hat. A yellow waning crescent moon hangs from strings behind him. The field where he walks is green and hilly. Seven spears stick up from the ground in the background. Tents and flags are also visible in the distance.

name: Eight of Swords
interpretation: A feat of concentration, a jester juggles seven swords while one more lays down safely at her feet.
image_description: Standing on one leg, a smiling jester juggles seven swords in a circle. An eighth sword lies on the ground. The swords all have red blades. The swords in the air have blue hilts, while the one on the ground has a green hilt. The jester has curly yellow hair under a green pointed cap with a white flower decoration and a bell on its tip. She also wears a green collar decorated with fluffy white flowers. She wears a green jumpsuit with a pattern of green, gold, red, and white leaves. Her shoes are purple with curled toes and bells.

name: Nine of Swords
interpretation: Removed from court life, a jester writes at a table in a small cottage looking out on a still pond. This could be a picturesque exile, but I also thought of how 19th century aristocrats paid faux hermits to live in tiny houses on their grounds and add an extra level of quaintness to their gardens.
image_description:
A jester sits in a small cottage, writing with a quill and looking out a window toward a pond.

In the picture, there are nine swords with yellow blades. Five swords form fence posts in front of the cottage. Three more swords are the trunks of trees in the background. A ninth sword lies at the bottom of the pond.

The pond is surrounded by tall yellow and green grasses, and a white goose stands at its edge. The one-room cottage has a thatched roof and a blue door with a pale blue diamond on its top panel and a yellow and red flower on its lower panel. The jester wears mainly white and sits by a red curtain.

name: Ten of Swords
interpretation: A jester discovers the puppet in a trunk, while the original waits just behind a curtain. Jodorowsky wrote that the final degree of Swords is the discovery of the Other – the person behind the ideas. Then, it is time to move into the emotional unity of Cups. This card is also a tribute to Raymond Roussel's novel Locus Solus, which got me started drawing clowns in the first place.
image_description: A kneeling jester lifts a puppet up out of a trunk. Another jester, who looks exactly like the puppet, peeks out from behind a curtain. This is the same winking jester who was painting the puppet in the Six of Swords. The jester who holds the puppet wears a white outfit with red and green stripes, red shoes with bells on the toes, and a three-pointed orange and green hat. Her hair is red and she has a blue collar. The trunk is purple, painted with flowers and a cup. The curtain that hangs behind the trunk is dark blue. In front of a curtain, there is a hanging picture of the Ten of Swords from the Marseille tarot.

name: Page of Swords
interpretation: The Page of Swords is based on Sir Jeffrey Hudson, a little person who found a place at French court and later had an even stranger life, going to war and being captured by pirates.
image_description: A jester in a doublet and puffed pants with a cockatoo on his shoulder unsheathes a sword. The jester smiles slightly and looks to his right. He holds the sheath in his right hand and holds the sword with the blade straight up in his left hand. The cockatoo sits on his left shoulder. He has a green feather stuck in his short yellow hair. His outfit is mainly red, with flowers and multicolored stripes on his doublet. He wears tall blue boots with bells on the ends of their curled toes. A red and green banner with gold spots hangs behind him.

name: Knight of Swords
interpretation: The Knight of Swords is based on João de Sá 'Panasco,' an African jester that rose in status in Portuguese court thanks to his military exploits.
image_description: A sword-wielding jester rides a horse, with a macaw flying behind him. The jester wears mostly black. His jacket has colorful stripes and a red cross on the sleeve, and his black leggings are decorated with yellow five-pointed stars. He also has a short red and blue cape and purple shoes with curled toes and bells. Bells swing from red and green ribbons attached to his saddle. He holds a red sword in his left hand. Facing the left side of the card, the horse jumps up on his hind legs. The horse is light blue with yellow mane and tail and a white star on his nose. He has a red bridle and an orange collar decorated with green stars.

name: Queen of Swords
interpretation: The Queen of Swords is based on Jamie Fleeman, a Scottish jester also worked on the family farm and helped defend the home.
image_description: A jester in overalls walks through fields, holding a lantern and accompanied by a raven. A yellow crescent moon hangs behind the clown from two strings, suggesting a play set. The clown holds a red sword in her right hand. The raven perches on her left hand where she holds the small red and yellow lantern. She has yellow braided hair under a large blue tam. Her overalls are green and covered in patches, and she also wears a floral shirt and big blue boots with untied yellow laces.

name: King of Swords
interpretation: The King of Swords is based on Stańczyk, the celebrated medieval Polish hero. This depiction in particular is from Jan Matejko's painting.
image_description: A jester holding a sword sits in a chair in front of a curtain, looking back over his shoulder. The jester wears mainly red. He has a three-pointed hat with bells and a short cloak over a tight-fitting outfit. His red legging are buttoned over his pointy blue shoes. He holds a red sword up in his right hand, and has a white recorder in his left hand. To his right, there is a green table with a letter and a quill pen in an inkwell. An orange and purple lute rests against the left side of his chair. A magpie perches on the back of the chair over the jester's left shoulder.
